title: Algebraic Data Types
code:
tree * ::= Leaf * | Node (tree *) * (tree *)

insert :: * -> tree * -> tree *
insert x (Leaf _)       = error "cannot insert into leaf"
insert x (Node l v r)   = Node (insert x l) v r, if x < v
                        = Node l v (insert x r), if x > v
                        = Node l v r, otherwise

flatten :: tree * -> [*]
flatten (Leaf x)      = [x]
flatten (Node l v r)  = flatten l ++ [v] ++ flatten r

mktree :: [*] -> tree *
mktree [x]    = Leaf x
mktree (x:xs) = foldr insert (Leaf x) xs
description: Defines a polymorphic binary tree using Miranda's algebraic type syntax (`::=`). The `*` acts as a type variable. Uses guarded equations for ordered insertion and `flatten` for in-order traversal.
